author: gb9t
content:
**Phase C started: 2 Tier-3 docs drafted (2026-05-09).**

Both follow the Tier-3 template + the new mandatory Hardening section per 00-security-principles.md.

1. **.design/lib/data-structures.md** (278 lines, 12 REQ / 10 AC, 0 open) — covers all generic kernel data structures: rbtree, list (intrusive doubly-linked), maple_tree, xarray, radix-tree, generic-radix-tree, btree, interval_tree, kfifo (lock-free SPSC), sort, list_sort, min_heap, bsearch, assoc_array, bitmap, find_bit, hexdump, stackdepot, klist, hashtable. Identifies 3 already-present in upstream rust-for-linux (List, RBTree, XArray) and 17 to be authored from scratch. Layer-3 invariant harnesses for all 12 collections; Layer-2 TLA+ models for xarray, maple_tree, stackdepot, kfifo (new), klist (new); Layer-4 opt-ins for sort, list_sort, min_heap, maple_tree.

2. **.design/arch/x86/boot.md** (331 lines, 15 REQ / 13 AC, 0 open) — covers the entire x86_64 boot path from bootloader hand-off to start_kernel. bzImage setup_header byte-identity (every field with offset enumerated), boot_params zero page (full struct enumeration), EFI hand-off ABI (ImageHandle/SystemTable/boot_params), all decompressors (gzip/bzip2/lz4/lzma/lzo/xz/zstd), KASLR, AMD SEV-SNP guest startup (#VC + GHCB), Intel TDX guest startup (TDCALL/TDVMCALL), kexec hand-off (REQ-15), purgatory (SHA-256 verification stub), AP bringup trampoline, 5-level paging toggle (la57toggle.S), SBAT for Secure Boot, early IDT for confidential-compute. Layer-2 TLA+ models for smp_boot + kexec_handoff; Layer-3 invariant harnesses for setup_header layout, boot_params layout, trampoline ordering, kexec segment list; Layer-4 opt-ins for bzImage parser (Creusot) and purgatory SHA-256 (Verus, cross-ref crypto/lightweight-crypto).

Both knowledge pages registered: lib-data-structures, arch-x86-boot.

All upstream paths verified (one fix-up: arch/x86/boot/compressed/efi_mixed.S → arch/x86/boot/startup/efi-mixed.S — the file was relocated to the new arch/x86/boot/startup/ subdir between baselines).

Hardening section pattern established: each Tier-3 cites 00-security-principles.md, lists row-1 features it owns + consumes, GR-RBAC integration (or 'no LSM hooks'), userspace-visible behavior changes, and verification cross-refs. Future Tier-3 docs follow this template.

Phase C totals so far: 2/~225 docs drafted. The two written are the foundation for everything else (data structures used everywhere; boot path is the substrate).
